title: Move source package to agent_takt and rewrite source imports
description: Rename `src/codex_orchestrator/` to `src/agent_takt/` and update Python import prefixes across source modules so intra-package imports resolve from `agent_takt` after the move.
acceptance_criteria: The package directory lives at `src/agent_takt/` and `src/codex_orchestrator/` is removed.
Source modules import from `agent_takt` instead of `codex_orchestrator`.
`from agent_takt.cli import main` imports successfully after the rename.
No `codex_orchestrator` import references remain in source `.py` modules outside intentionally preserved historical text.

handoff_summary.completed:
- Copied src/codex_orchestrator/ → src/agent_takt/ preserving all module and _data content
- Updated _assets.py: changed files("codex_orchestrator") to files("agent_takt") and updated docstrings
- Updated merge-conflict.md template: changed import check command to reference agent_takt
- Updated planner-decomposition SKILL.md: changed example path from src/codex_orchestrator/ to src/agent_takt/
- Removed src/codex_orchestrator/ entirely
- Verified: `from agent_takt.cli import main` imports successfully
- Committed as [bead] B-3f6ffee3
handoff_summary.risks: Tests in tests/ still import from codex_orchestrator (301 occurrences across 15 files). These must be updated by the downstream tester/documentation beads or a dedicated bead for test import rewrites.
handoff_summary.next_action: Update all test files (15 files, 301 occurrences) that import from codex_orchestrator to import from agent_takt instead.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Downstream beads that touch test files will encounter failing imports until tests are updated to reference agent_takt instead of codex_orchestrator. The tester bead must address this before the test suite can pass.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Chose cp -r then rm -rf rather than git mv because git mv doesn't handle directory renames with nested _data trees cleanly across all git versions. Git detected the renames automatically (similarity index 100% for unchanged files, ~80-97% for modified ones). The only Python source change was in _assets.py: the importlib.resources files() call, which is the sole runtime reference to the package name as a string. Two bundled markdown files also referenced the old name in code examples and were updated for correctness.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: The tester bead must first update all 301 import references across 15 test files (codex_orchestrator → agent_takt) before any tests can run. Key test files: test_assets.py (importlib.resources path), test_config.py, test_orchestrator.py (72 occurrences), test_onboarding.py, and others. After updating imports, the tester should verify: (1) from agent_takt.cli import main works, (2) _assets.py asset paths resolve correctly, (3) all existing test scenarios still pass.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Test files were intentionally not modified — they are the tester agent's responsibility. The 301 test import references to codex_orchestrator will cause all tests to fail until updated.

conflict_risks: Downstream beads that touch test files will encounter failing imports until tests are updated to reference agent_takt instead of codex_orchestrator. The tester bead must address this before the test suite can pass.

execution_history.summary: Renamed src/codex_orchestrator/ to src/agent_takt/ and updated all codex_orchestrator references in source Python modules and bundled data files. The importlib.resources lookup in _assets.py now resolves to agent_takt. Import verified with `from agent_takt.cli import main`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
